summary: The Docker build failed because a wget download of the nydus release returned HTTP 502, causing the build step to exit with code 1 and buildx bake to fail.
root_cause: A network/remote-server error occurred: wget returned "HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway" when attempting to download the nydus release, causing the build step to exit with code 1 and abort the build.

evidence.quote: wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: Shows the wget command failed due to an HTTP 502 response from the remote server.

evidence.quote: #72 ERROR: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Shows the specific build step invoking wget failed and exited with code 1.

evidence.quote: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Indicates buildkit failed to solve the build because the wget step failed.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: CI runner reports that buildx bake failed due to the wget failure, confirming this caused the job failure.

suggested_fix: Retry the CI run (transient 502). If the failure persists, make the build resilient to remote failures by caching or vendoring the nydus artifact, adding retry logic around the wget, or hosting a reliable mirror for the release URL.

summary: A functional test failed because pip could not download a GitHub archive: repeated 502 responses caused the install step to error and the test to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The test tests/functional/test_truststore.py::test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub] failed because pip was unable to fetch the GitHub-hosted package: HTTPSConnectionPool to host='github.com' returned repeated 502 errors and max retries were exceeded, causing the install command to exit with code 1.

evidence.reason: This is the failing test identifier reported by pytest.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Shows pip failed to download from GitHub due to repeated 502 responses, which explains the install failure.
The test harness reports the script exited with a non-zero status, consistent with the failed download/installation.

evidence.quote: ^[[36mnox > ^[[31mCommand pytest tests/functional --verbose --numprocesses auto --showlocals --durations=15 failed with exit code 1^[[0m
evidence.reason: The overall functional test session failed as a result of the test error.

suggested_fix: Treat this as a transient network failure: re-run the CI job. If failures recur, make the test independent of live GitHub (mock the download or use a local test server/fixture), or add handling/increased retries in test setup to avoid flaky external network dependencies.

summary: The test run failed with one failing test suite; logs show a config env-variable replacement warning from src/getConfig.ts and the test command exited with code 1.
root_cause: A Jest test suite failed during CI (1 failed). The logs include a warning from src/getConfig.ts about failing to replace an environment variable (${ENV_VAR_123}), indicating the test encountered unexpected config/env behavior and caused the test run to exit non-zero.
relevant_tests: Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total

evidence.quote: Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
evidence.reason: Shows that one test suite failed in the Jest run.
Identifies a warning originating from src/getConfig.ts about failing to replace an env var, which is likely related to the test failure.
Shows the test script (jest) failed under pnpm, indicating the test run aborted with an error.
Confirms the test command exited with a non-zero status, causing the CI job to fail.
An explicit error present in the logs that may be part of the failing test behavior or related setup error observed during the run.

suggested_fix: Run the failing test locally with the same environment to reproduce; ensure the environment variable ENV_VAR_123 is set or mock the env replacement in the test (or adjust src/getConfig.ts to handle missing env vars without warning). Re-run jest and verify the suite passes before pushing.
